sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a frequent water skier, experienced a knee pain after a skiing incident on Lake Martin, where he twisted his right knee while landing from a jump, resulting in pain and swelling.
He reports no hearing or feeling of a pop, but describes the knee as sore to squat and bend.
He denies any prior knee injuries and has been managing his high blood pressure with lisinopril 20mg daily.
He reports taking over-the-counter Aleve twice daily and applying a cold pack for pain relief.
He denies numbness or tingling in his foot.
Past medical history: The patient has had a tonsillectomy and is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
He reports checking his blood pressure at home, with readings of 125/80 most days.
Review of systems: The patient denies any symptoms in other organs or systems.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis and edema on the right knee, with an effusion present.
Palpation of the right medial knee is painful.
The patient has decreased range of motion, negative varus and valgus test, and negative Lachman sign.
The dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses are palpable.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee; Hypertension; Past medical history of tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any broken bones.
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension and ordered a lipid panel to check for any potential issues.
I also administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen after the results of the x-ray are obtained and after the lipid panel is completed.
